scope: AgenC daemon socket authentication for local clients using a private shared-cookie initialize gate when portable Unix peer credentials are unavailable in Node.
_sourceRootBrandingAllow: branding-scan: allow donor source root path

requiredBehaviors: create a daemon cookie when none exists
reuse an existing non-empty daemon cookie
write daemon cookie files with private permissions
repair permissive existing cookie-file permissions before reuse
edgeCases: existing cookie with loose file permissions

requiredBehaviors: reject missing, empty, wrong, and length-mismatched initialize cookies
use Node's constant-time equality primitive for same-length cookie comparisons
route foreground daemon initialize authentication through the shared verifier
edgeCases: wrong cookie cannot reach daemon auth methods
wrong cookie closes the persistent daemon socket after the initialize failure
missing initialize cookie fails before follow-up requests
empty expected daemon cookie is rejected at startup

requiredBehaviors: document that AgenC uses the D-14 shared-cookie route instead of a non-portable Node peer-credential probe
keep Unix socket file and directory permissions private
do not invent an undocumented native peer-credential binding
edgeCases: Windows has no Unix socket transport
